steps: Sign in with your Google account
Click 'Create Project' (or select an existing project from the dropdown)
In the left sidebar, click 'OAuth consent screen' and configure it (choose External or Internal, fill in app name and email)
Go back to 'Credentials', click '+ Create Credentials', then select 'OAuth client ID'
Choose 'Web application' as the application type
Add your redirect URI (e.g. http://localhost for local testing)
Click 'Create' — copy the Client ID and Client Secret
Enable the Google Ads API: go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/googleads.googleapis.com and click 'Enable'
You also need a Google Ads Manager Account (MCC) developer token: visit https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/access-levels and apply

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console

notes: Requires a Google Ads account and a developer token (apply at https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/access-levels)
Developer token starts with Test access (limited) — apply for Basic access for production
The Google Ads API scope is: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords
Manager accounts (MCC) are required for accessing multiple customer accounts
API requests also require a 'developer-token' header and optionally a 'login-customer-id' header
